title: Diplazium mollifrons
taxonomy: Diplazium mollifrons is classified within the family Athyriaceae, which is a group of ferns commonly known as lady ferns or marsh ferns. This family is part of the order Polypodiales, one of the most diverse groups of ferns. The family Athyriaceae also includes other genera such as Athyrium and Deparia. The classification of Diplazium mollifrons is further refined at the genus and species levels. This specific classification helps to identify and understand the unique characteristics of Diplazium mollifrons within the plant kingdom.

intro: Diplazium mollifrons, also known as the compound fern or mole-leaf fern, is a species of fern in the family Athyriaceae. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine, offering various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antiseptic properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of colds, fever, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Diplazium mollifrons include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and sesquiterpenes, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Diplazium mollifrons is commonly prepared as a decoction or infusion, and is often used to make cough syrups, teas, and tinctures to treat respiratory and digestive issues.
